description: Learn Python from your current level to building real projects

assessment.beginner_code:
name = input('What is your name?')
print('Hello ' + name)
assessment.beginner_explanation: Takes input from user and prints a greeting message
assessment.intermediate_code:
numbers = [1, 2, 3, 4, 5]
squares = [x**2 for x in numbers if x % 2 == 0]
print(squares)
assessment.intermediate_explanation: Creates a list of squares of only even numbers using list comprehension
assessment.advanced_explanation: A Stack data structure implemented using a Python class with push pop and empty check methods

complete_beginner.label: Complete Beginner
complete_beginner.description: You are new to programming entirely

beginner.label: Beginner
beginner.description: You understand basics but not intermediate concepts

intermediate.label: Intermediate
intermediate.description: You understand basics and some intermediate concepts

milestones.title: Python Basics
milestones.description: Variables, data types, operators, input and output
topics: Type conversion — int to string etc

free_resources.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners by CodeWithHarry

project.title: Simple Calculator
project.description: Take two numbers as input from user and perform addition subtraction multiplication and division. Show results clearly.

free_resources.title: Control Flow in Python by CodeWithHarry
Python Control Flow by Corey Schafer

project.title: Number Guessing Game
project.description: Computer picks a random number between 1 and 100. User keeps guessing. Tool says too high or too low until user gets it right. Show number of attempts.

milestones.title: Functions and Modules
milestones.description: Write reusable code with functions and use built in modules
topics: Built in modules — math random datetime os

project.title: Unit Converter
project.description: Convert km to miles celsius to fahrenheit kg to pounds and INR to USD. Each conversion should be its own function. User picks what to convert.

milestones.title: Data Structures
topics: Tuples and when to use them

free_resources.title: Data Structures in Python by CodeWithHarry

project.title: Contact Book
project.description: Store names phone numbers and emails. Add new contact. Search by name. Delete contact. Show all contacts. Save to memory using dictionary.

milestones.title: File Handling and Error Handling
milestones.description: Read and write files and handle errors gracefully

free_resources.title: File Handling in Python by CodeWithHarry
Python File Objects by Corey Schafer

project.title: Todo List App
project.description: Add tasks mark them complete delete them and show all tasks. Save everything to a file so tasks persist when you restart the program.

free_resources.title: OOP in Python by Corey Schafer

project.title: Bank Account System
project.description: Create a BankAccount class. Methods for deposit withdraw check balance and transaction history. Create multiple accounts. One account can transfer to another.

milestones.title: Libraries and APIs
milestones.description: Use external libraries and connect to real world APIs

free_resources.title: Python Requests Library by Corey Schafer
Web Scraping with Python by CodeWithHarry

project.title: Weather App
project.description: Ask user for city name. Fetch real weather data from OpenWeatherMap free API. Show temperature humidity wind speed and conditions. Handle errors if city not found.

milestones.title: Final Capstone Project
milestones.description: Build a complete real world project and put it on GitHub
topics: Project planning and breaking into tasks
Git basics — init add commit push
Basic deployment on Streamlit or Render

project.title: Build Your Own Tool
project.description: Pick any problem you face daily and solve it with Python. Could be a expense tracker, a reminder system, a file organiser, anything. Deploy it. Share the GitHub link.
